text:
In the bustling city park on a lazy Saturday afternoon, Luna the owl sat on a high branch, eyeing the sky curiously. She had caught a swarm of curious beetles. They buzzed around her, attracted by her eerie yet captivating calls.

"Lou-laaaa," she murmured melodiously.

The beetles fluttered closer, intrigued. Luna opened her beak wide, laughing softly. "Oh, don't be frightened. I don't bite, much."

One beetle landed on her wing.

 *"Just say it quick, will you?"*

"I love you! Now buzz away!"

The beetles formed silly shapes in the air, spinning like autumn leaves. Luna bobbed her head contentedly.

Just as they reached their artistic height, a fox jumped out from behind a hollow oak.

 *"Well, Luna," the fox said, "that was quite an affair."

*"You do have quite a personality,"* Luna said.

*The fox smirked.*

 *"Come here,*
